---
title: "Ubuntu laptop sometimes fails to wake up from sleep mode"
date: 2023-03-24
forum: General Help
---

### Post by a-random-username on 2023-03-24
I have an  Origin laptop with Ubuntu 22.04.2 LTS. I can usually put the laptop in  sleep mode and wake it back up. But about one in every 4 times, I wake  the laptop up, I can use it for about 15 seconds (even going to a  website for example) and then it completely freezes. I can't move the  mouse, I can't switch to a virtual terminal.
 There is nothing in `/var/crash/`. The `syslog` shows this for a  power down and booting that leads to a crashed restart (not exaggerating  with the 00\00s, this is a copy paste):
     ```
Mar 24 15:35:44 name-EVO15-S wpa_supplicant[1004]: nl80211: deinit ifname=p2p-dev-wlp0s20 disabled_11b_rates=0
    Mar 24 15:35:44 name-EVO15-S systemd[1]: Reached target Sleep.
    Mar 24 15:35:44 name-EVO15-S systemd[1]: Starting Record successful boot for GRUB...
    Mar 24 15:35:44 name-EVO15-S systemd[1]: Starting System Suspend...
    Mar 24 15:35:44 name-EVO15-S systemd-sleep[15280]: Entering sleep state 'suspend'...

```


 And then it continues from there, booting up the CPU and other IO.  That long `00\00\00` string as it's going to sleep looks suspicious,  since that always leads to a problem when I wake back up. But what could  be causing that? It happens at the equivalent place of the `Starting  GRUB failed boot detection...` line in the working one. Is GRUB boot  detection failing somehow in the Standby routine? What is `inserted  module 'lp'`? What can I do about that?

### Post by MAFoElffen on 2023-03-25
Thea littlest of information, at the least would be helpful:
```

grep 'model name' /proc/cpuinfo | head -n 1
uname -r
sudo lshw -C video

```

### Post by MAFoElffen on 2023-03-30
The NVidia GeForce RTX 3080 Mobile GPU is too new to use the Nouveau driver. I'm surprised that you were not getting DRM errors. 

Try going to Additional Drivers to install a third party NVidia driver from the Repo...

---
